perturbation.original_text: The undersigned hereby agree that the Schedule 13G/A with respect to the shares of Class A Common Stock, $0.001 par value per share, of The RMR Group Inc., dated as of December 31, 2019, is, and any amendments thereto (including amendments on Schedule 13D) signed by each of the undersigned shall be, filed on behalf of each of us pursuant to and in accordance with the provisions of Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned hereby agree that the Schedule 13G/A with respect to the shares of Class A Common Stock, $0.001 par value per share, of The RMR Group Inc., dated as of December 31, 2019, may be, and any amendments thereto (including amendments on Schedule 13D) signed by each of the undersigned may be, filed on behalf of each of us pursuant to and in accordance with the provisions of Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "is" to "may be", and "shall be" to "may be", we introduce uncertainty as to whether filing is mandatory or optional. This directly contradicts the initial agreement that filing *is* required, creating ambiguity about the obligations under Rule 13d-1(k).
perturbation.contradicted_text: Exhibit 99.1 JOINT FILING AGREEMENT The undersigned hereby agree that the Schedule 13G/A with respect to the shares of Class A Common Stock, $0.001 par value per share, of The RMR Group Inc., dated as of December 31, 2019, may be, and any amendments thereto (including amendments on Schedule 13D) signed by each of the undersigned may be, filed on behalf of each of us pursuant to and in accordance with the provisions of Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended. January 22, 2020 (Date) ABP TRUST /s/ Adam D. Portnoy (Signature) Adam D. Portnoy, President (Name/Title) ADAM D. PORTNOY /s/ Adam D. Portnoy (Signature) 10
perturbation.justification: The original text states that filing 'is' and 'shall be' done, indicating a mandatory obligation. The changed text replaces 'is' with 'may be' and 'shall be' with 'may be,' making the filing optional. This directly contradicts the initial requirement of mandatory filing, causing uncertainty regarding compliance with Rule 13d-1(k). Because the contradicted text exhibits the effect of the change, by reflecting what was originally changed, the change therefore induces a contradiction.

perturbation.explanation: Changing the date from January 22, 2020, to January 23, 2020, creates a contradiction as the agreement states it is dated as of December 31, 2019, but signed on January 22, 2020. This date inconsistency creates uncertainty regarding the agreement's validity and enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Exhibit 99.1 JOINT FILING AGREEMENT The undersigned hereby agree that the Schedule 13G/A with respect to the shares of Class A Common Stock, $0.001 par value per share, of The RMR Group Inc., dated as of December 31, 2019, may be, and any amendments thereto (including amendments on Schedule 13D) signed by each of the undersigned may be, filed on behalf of each of us pursuant to and in accordance with the provisions of Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended. January 23, 2020 (Date) ABP TRUST /s/ Adam D. Portnoy (Signature) Adam D. Portnoy, President (Name/Title) ADAM D. PORTNOY /s/ Adam D. Portnoy (Signature) 10
perturbation.justification: The changed date from January 22, 2020 to January 23, 2020 in the first section contradicts the 'Exhibit 99.1 JOINT FILING AGREEMENT' section. Both sections now state different dates, with the Joint Filing Agreement referencing both December 31, 2019, and January 23, 2020. This creates inconsistency about the date of the agreement, leading to potential issues regarding validity and enforceability. The dates do not align, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: ABP TRUST /s/ Adam D. Portnoy (Signature) Adam D. Portnoy, President (Name/Title)
perturbation.changed_text: ABP TRUST /s/ Adam D. Portnoy (Signature) Adam D. Portnoy, Secretary (Name/Title)
perturbation.explanation: Changing Adam D. Portnoy's title from "President" to "Secretary" introduces a potential contradiction because, legally, it is unclear if a Secretary is authorized to sign the agreement on behalf of the ABP Trust. This creates uncertainty regarding the enforceability and who is liable for the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Exhibit 99.1 JOINT FILING AGREEMENT The undersigned hereby agree that the Schedule 13G/A with respect to the shares of Class A Common Stock, $0.001 par value per share, of The RMR Group Inc., dated as of December 31, 2019, may be, and any amendments thereto (including amendments on Schedule 13D) signed by each of the undersigned may be, filed on behalf of each of us pursuant to and in accordance with the provisions of Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended. January 23, 2020 (Date) ABP TRUST /s/ Adam D. Portnoy (Signature) Adam D. Portnoy, Secretary (Name/Title) ADAM D. PORTNOY /s/ Adam D. Portnoy (Signature) 10
perturbation.justification: The change from 'President' to 'Secretary' for Adam D. Portnoy's title introduces uncertainty regarding his authority to sign on behalf of ABP Trust. The contradicted text shows him signing as 'Secretary,' but this change creates ambiguity about whether that role is authorized to bind the Trust, thus contradicting the implicit assumption of authorized signature within the Joint Filing Agreement. The explanation states that "legally, it is unclear if a Secretary is authorized to sign the agreement on behalf of the ABP Trust", thus leading to a contradiction.
